run_name: NATP Module 17: Patient/Resident Abuse — Source-First Dry Run
purpose: The purpose of this unit is to introduce the Nurse Assistant to patient/resident abuse. The module will focus on the nurse assistant role in preventing, recognizing, and reporting instances of patient/resident abuse.

objectives.objective_title: Identify and discuss types of elder abuse
objectives.exact_objective_statement: Identify and discuss types of elder abuse
objectives.source_excerpt: A. Lecture/Discussion B. Class participation Identify and discuss types of elder abuse B. Arrange for tour of a A. Define Abuse long term care facility. 1. An intentional act or failure to act by a caregiver or another C. Arrange for a guest person in a relationship involving an expectation of trust that causes or creates a risk of harm to an older adult who speaker from a long is someone age 60 or older term care facility. 2. A crime and is one or more of the following elements: 1) Willful causing of injury 2) Unreasonable confinement 3) Intimidation, making someone afraid 4) Punishment 5) Refusal of goods or services needed for physical, mental or psychological well-being 3. Can happen to any older person, but often affects those who depend on others for help with activities of everyday life 4. Likely targets are older people who have no family or Module 17: Patient/Resident Abuse problems, cognitive problems, or dementia B. Forms or types of Elder Abuse (National Institute of Aging): 1. Verbal 1) Teasing 2) Profanity 3) Racial slanders 4) Threats 2. Physical 1) Battery: kicking, biting, hitting, shoving, pulling hair 2) Rough handling 3. Psychological (Emotional abuse) 1) Ridiculing or saying hurtful words 2) Ignoring repeatedly 3) Manipulating 4) Yelling at or threatening 4. Involuntary seclusion 1) Isolation 2) Keeping that person from seeing close friends and relatives 5. Abandonment 1) Leaving someone unattended 2) No planning for patient's/resident's care 6. Neglect 1) Failure to provide care that a reasonable person would provide 2) Not answering call light 3) Smel

objectives.objective_title: Explain the Nurse Assistant role in preventing elder abuse
objectives.exact_objective_statement: Explain the Nurse Assistant role in preventing elder abuse
objectives.source_excerpt: Explain the Nurse Assistant role in recognizing and preventing elder abuse Module 17: Patient/Resident Abuse A. Explain the difference between negligence and abuse A. Lecture/Discussion A. Written test B. Recognize the evidence of elder abuse B. Locate facility policies B. Class participation C. Locate facility policies, 1. Living conditions are unsafe, unclean or inadequate. and procedures and 2. Personal hygiene is lacking. patient/resident rights procedures, and 3. Weight loss documents. patient/resident rights 4. Frequent injuries C. Role-play a situation that documents. 5. Old bruises involves a threat to D. Consistently promotes 6. Quiet and withdrawn attitude patient/resident rights patient/resident rights 7. Anxious to please caregiver 8. Private conversations not allowed 9. Medications not taken properly 10. Frequent Visits to ER, hospital, or clinics 11. May go from one doctor to another C. Explain Nurse Assistant role in preventing abuse 1. Identify combination of individual, relational, community, and societal factors contribute to the risk of abuse 2. Current diagnosis of mental illness 3. Current abuse of alcohol 4. High levels of hostility 5. Poor or inadequate preparation or training for care giving responsibilities 6. Assumption of caregiving responsibilities at an early age 7. Inadequate coping skills 8. Exposure to abuse as a child 9. High financial and emotional dependence upon a vulnerable elder 10. Lack of support 11. Unsympathetic or negative attitudes toward patients/residents 12. Chronic staffing problems 13. Lack of administrative oversight, staff
